Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.
